``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Florida has one of the nation’s most unvarying determinate systems with no parolable sentences of any kind. All life sentences are life terms without the possibility of parole. Even with gain time incarcerated people's earliest possible release dates occur at the 85-percent mark of their maximum terms.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 23%, changing from 104,306 in 2010 to 80,417 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity
## Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 19%, dropping from 36,239 in 2010 to 29,264 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 11% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 0% increase compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Florida. The largest change was for robbery offenses, which increased by 57%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Florida, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 4.9 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Hispanic, any race individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 3.3 years. White, non-Hispanic individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 2.7 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Rape or Sexual Assault offenses, Black, non-Hispanic individuals had 3.04 more years on average compared to Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
